body.title: CLOSED: unify inertia tier table into inertia_map.py
body.result: inertia_map.py created; scope_guard, self_edit, skill_filter, pe_chain all delegate to it; parity test added; 4405 tests pass
body.description:
scope_guard._TIER_TABLE (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW bucket, prefix-string) and self_edit.INERTIA (float weights 0.0-1.0) encode the same inertia policy with different shapes and different entries — two copies of a load-bearing safety contract drifting apart. pe_filter also has its own hardcoded HIGH-inertia check duplicating scope_guard. Fix: create wild_igor/igor/tools/inertia_map.py with a single ordered table and helpers: bucket_of(path) -> HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW, weight_of(path) -> float. Both scope_guard.py and self_edit.py import from it. pe_filter delegates to scope_guard.classify_path(). Day-1 behavior identical — audit for equivalence first, then flip imports.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/scope_guard.py, wild_igor/igor/tools/self_edit.py, new wild_igor/igor/tools/inertia_map.py, pe_filter (whereabouts in pe_chain.py), tests/test_scope_guard.py
**Design rules:** none-apply
**Scope boundary:** IN: unify two tables into one module; wire all three callers to it. OUT: do not change effective tiers applied anywhere on day 1; graph-derived inertia is future work.
**Test plan:** existing test_scope_guard.py must pass unchanged; add equivalence test that bucket_of + weight_of return same values as old tables for all existing paths.
**Pass condition:** grep for _TIER_TABLE outside inertia_map.py returns nothing; grep for INERTIA dict outside inertia_map.py returns nothing; pytest passes.
